methods.description: Process for extracting lipids from diatom cultures, separating neutral lipids using thin layer chromatography (TLC) with flame ionization detection (FID), detecting them, estimating triacylglycerols (TAGs) amount using a calibration curve, and calculating lipid productivity.

steps.description: Diatom cultures of 250 mL were harvested by centrifugation at 3500 g for 20 min.
steps.searchable_summary: harvest diatom cultures by centrifugation

steps.description: Pellets were immediately resuspended in 2-3 mL of a chloroform/methanol solution (2:1, v/v) plus 0.2 mL of 1 N HCl and kept at −20 • C in glass vials.
steps.searchable_summary: resuspend cell pellets in solvent mixture and store

steps.description: To complete lipid extraction, samples were mixed with one volume of ultrapure H 2 O and vortexed vigorously.
steps.searchable_summary: mix samples with water and vortex for lipid extraction

steps.description: The mixtures were centrifuged at 1000× g for 5 min at 4 • C to fully separate the two phases.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge mixture to separate organic and aqueous phases

steps.description: The lower (organic) phase was recovered
steps.searchable_summary: recover the lower organic phase

steps.description: and washed once with a NaCl-saturated solution,
steps.searchable_summary: wash organic phase with NaCl solution

steps.description: followed by centrifugation at 3500× g for 20 min
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge washed organic phase

steps.description: and the organic phase was recovered again.
steps.searchable_summary: recover the organic phase after washing and centrifugation

steps.description: The lipid-containing organic phase was finally transfer to a glass vial for storage (−20 • C).
steps.searchable_summary: transfer lipid extract to vial for storage

steps.description: Neutral lipids were separated by thin layer chromatography on silica-coated rods (SIII Chromarods)
steps.searchable_summary: separate neutral lipids using thin layer chromatography (TLC)

steps.description: and detected by flame ionization using a MK-6 Iatroscan TLC-FID apparatus (Iatron Laboratories, Tokyo, Japan) as described previously in [36].
steps.searchable_summary: detect separated lipids using flame ionization detection (FID)

steps.description: Data acquisition and processing was performed with the i-Chromstar 6.3 integration software (SCPA GmbH, Bremen, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: acquire and process TLC-FID data using chromatography software

steps.description: The amount of TAGs was estimated from a calibration curve.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate TAGs amount using a calibration curve
inputs: Processed chromatographic data (e.g., TAG peak area)

steps.description: Lipid productivity (mg TAGs L −1 day −1 ) was calculated as P biomass ·Cf, where P biomass is the biomass productivity, calculated as shown above, and Cf corresponds to the final concentration of TAGs at exponential or stationary phase.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate lipid productivity using biomass productivity and final TAG concentration

methods.description: Procedure to measure GAPDH enzyme activity by following the consumption of NADPH at 340 nm using a UV/VIS spectrophotometer in a coupled assay, including the synthesis of the substrate bisphosphoglyceric acid (BPGA).

steps.description: BPGA synthesis was performed as follows: a reaction containing phosphoglyceric acid (PGA; 66 mM), 33 mM ATP and~4.5 units of phosphoglycerate kinase (PGK) was incubated in glycyl-glycine buffer (50 mM glycyl-glycine, 0.5 mM EDTA, 15 mM MgCl 2 , 50 mM KCl, pH 7.7) for 20 min at 30 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: synthesize bisphosphoglyceric acid (BPGA) using PGA, ATP, and PGK

steps.description: To determine GAPDH activity the consumption of NADPH to NADP + (ε 340 nm = 6 220 M −1 cm −1 ) was followed using a Perkin Elmer Lambda 25 UV/VIS spectrophotometer (Waltham, MA, USA) at 340 nm in a reaction mix containing 50 µL of the freshly prepared BPGA, 0.2 mM NADPH, and 2-20 µL of protein crude extract in a total volume of 1 mL in buffer.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare GAPDH assay reaction mix

steps.description: Additionally, dithiothreitol (DTT) might be added at 5 mM final concentration for redox assays.
steps.searchable_summary: optionally add DTT to GAPDH assay reaction mix
outputs: GAPDH assay reaction mix (with or without DTT)

steps.description: the consumption of NADPH to NADP + (ε 340 nm = 6 220 M −1 cm −1 ) was followed using a Perkin Elmer Lambda 25 UV/VIS spectrophotometer (...) at 340 nm
steps.searchable_summary: measure NADPH consumption rate at 340 nm using UV/VIS spectrophotometry
outputs: Spectrophotometric data (Absorbance at 340 nm over time)

steps.description: To determine GAPDH activity the consumption of NADPH to NADP + (ε 340 nm = 6 220 M −1 cm −1 ) was followed
steps.searchable_summary: calculate GAPDH activity from NADPH consumption rate using extinction coefficient
inputs: Spectrophotometric data (Absorbance at 340 nm over time)
NADPH extinction coefficient (ε 340 nm = 6220 M−1 cm−1)

methods.description: Indirect measurement of PGK enzyme activity by coupling its reaction to the NADH-consuming GAPDH reaction and following NADH consumption at 340 nm using a UV/VIS spectrophotometer.

steps.description: The reaction mix contained 1 mM ATP, 2 mM PGA, 0.15 mM NADH, and 0.5 unit of rabbit GAPDH in a total volume of 1 mL.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare PGK assay reaction mix for coupled assay

steps.description: PGK activity was related to NADH consumption followed at 340 nm as previously.
steps.searchable_summary: measure NADH consumption rate at 340 nm using UV/VIS spectrophotometry
outputs: Spectrophotometric data (Absorbance at 340 nm over time)

steps.description: PGK activity was related to NADH consumption followed at 340 nm as previously.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate PGK activity from NADH consumption rate
inputs: Spectrophotometric data (Absorbance at 340 nm over time)

methods.description: Measurement of PK enzyme activity by coupling its reaction to the NADH-consuming lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) reaction and following NADH consumption at 340 nm using a UV/VIS spectrophotometer.

steps.description: The reaction mix contained 5 mM PEP, 0.2 mM NADH, 2 units of LDH, and 1 mM ADP in a total volume of 1 mL,
steps.searchable_summary: prepare PK assay reaction mix for coupled assay

steps.description: and NADH consumption was followed at 340 nm.
steps.searchable_summary: measure NADH consumption rate at 340 nm using UV/VIS spectrophotometry
outputs: Spectrophotometric data (Absorbance at 340 nm over time)
